ev2go/ev2go.io.rationale: ev2go.io is an iOS mobile app for electric vehicle charging station management, labeled 'Only for iPhone' and 'Not verified for macOS' on the App Store. It is not a macOS desktop application and would not appear in typical Fleet enterprise customer device inventories.

evershop/evershop.rationale: EverShop is a Node.js/TypeScript server-side e-commerce platform requiring PostgreSQL; it has no Homebrew formula/cask, no macOS installer, and is not a desktop application appropriate for a macOS desktop inventory.

eyoucms/eyoucms.rationale: EyouCMS is a Chinese PHP-based web CMS (enterprise website builder) with no macOS desktop application or Homebrew package. It is a server-side web application unlikely to appear in any Western Fleet customer's inventory.

fabian/contact_management_system.rationale: The 'fabian' Contact Management System is a PHP/MySQL web application from code-projects (fabianros) that runs on a web server — it has no macOS installer, no Homebrew package, and is not a native desktop application.

fabian/online_application_system_for_adm.rationale: This is a PHP/MySQL web application (a school admission management system from code-projects.org) designed to run on a LAMP/XAMPP stack, not a macOS desktop application. There is no Homebrew package, .pkg, or .dmg installer; it is server-side web software not relevant to a macOS desktop inventory.

fabian/online_examination_system.rationale: Fabian Online Examination System is a PHP-based web application (server-side), not a macOS desktop or CLI tool. It has no Homebrew formula or cask, and is not appropriate for installation on a macOS desktop VM.

fabian/online_music_site.rationale: 'Online Music Site' by code-projects (NVD vendor 'fabian') is a PHP-based web application with SQL injection CVEs, not a macOS desktop application. It has no Homebrew package and no macOS installer.

fabian/online_product_reservation_system.rationale: This is a PHP-based web application from code-projects.org, not a macOS desktop application. It runs as a server-side web app with no Homebrew package or macOS installer available.

fabian/online_reviewer_system.rationale: The 'Online Reviewer System' is a PHP web application from code-projects.org with SQL injection CVEs; 'fabian' is the security researcher who reported them, not the vendor. It is a server-side web app with no macOS desktop installer.

fabian/online_student_management_system.rationale: The 'online_student_management_system' by 'fabian' is a PHP web application (code-projects), not a macOS desktop application. It is not available via Homebrew and has no macOS installer.

fabian/simple_blood_donor_management_sys.rationale: Simple Blood Donor Management System is a PHP/MySQL web application (vulnerability is in /simpleblooddonor/editcampaignform.php), not a macOS desktop application. It is a code-projects student project with no Homebrew presence, no macOS installer, and is not appropriate for macOS desktop inventory.

facturascripts/facturascripts.rationale: FacturaScripts is a Spanish-market PHP web application with no Homebrew formula/cask, no .pkg or .dmg installer — it requires a local web server stack (ServBay/XAMPP). As a regional Spanish accounting web app, it is unlikely to appear in Fleet customer device inventories.

fastgpt/fastgpt.rationale: FastGPT (labring/FastGPT, matching version scheme 4.x.x.x consistent with CVE-2026-34162 fixed in <4.14.9.5) is a server-side Docker-based knowledge/RAG platform deployed via docker compose, not a macOS desktop application. No Homebrew cask or formula exists, and there is no macOS installer.

feathersjs/feathers.rationale: FeathersJS/Feathers is a Node.js server-side API framework installed exclusively via npm (`npm install @feathersjs/feathers`), not as a standalone macOS application. No Homebrew formula or cask exists, and no .pkg/.dmg installer is available.

feminer/warehouse_management_system.rationale: FeMiner WMS is a PHP-based web application (CVE references the /src/chkuser.php endpoint), making it server-side software with no macOS desktop presence. It is not available via Homebrew and has no macOS installer.

fleetdm/fleet.rationale: FleetDM Fleet is a Linux-based server application for device management; its macOS releases are only CLI archives (fleetctl tar.gz/zip) with no .pkg or .dmg, and all deployment guides target cloud/container infrastructure rather than macOS desktops.

flowiseai/flowise.rationale: Flowise is a Node.js server-side web application (installed via npm or Docker) with no Homebrew formula/cask, no .pkg or .dmg installer, and no native macOS desktop distribution — it does not fit any of the supported macOS install methods.

flowring/agentflow.rationale: Flowring Agentflow is a Java-based, server-side BPM/workflow management enterprise system primarily marketed in Taiwan and China, accessed via web browser with no macOS desktop installer. It is a regional enterprise server application not appropriate for a macOS desktop install recipe.

fortinet/fortianalyzer.rationale: FortiAnalyzer is a Fortinet enterprise security log management and analytics appliance deployed exclusively as a hardware device or virtual machine (VMware, Hyper-V, KVM). It has no macOS desktop installer and is not appropriate for installation on a macOS test VM.

fortinet/forticlientems.rationale: FortiClientEMS is a server-side endpoint management platform that runs only on Windows Server or Linux (Ubuntu, RHEL, CentOS). It has no macOS support and is not a desktop application.

fortinet/fortimanager.rationale: FortiManager is an enterprise network management platform deployed as a VM or physical appliance, not a macOS desktop application. Fortinet only provides VM install guides (VMware, KVM, etc.) with no macOS client or desktop app available.

fortinet/fortiproxy.rationale: FortiProxy is a Fortinet enterprise secure web gateway/proxy appliance that runs on dedicated hardware or virtual appliances (FortiOS-based), not a macOS desktop application. It has no Homebrew presence and no macOS installer; it should not be confused with FortiClient (the VPN client).

fortinet/fortisandbox.rationale: FortiSandbox is a network security appliance/virtual machine (VMware ESXi, KVM, Hyper-V, Nutanix) for malware sandboxing — it is not a macOS desktop application and has no Homebrew formula, cask, or macOS installer.

fortinet/fortisandboxcloud.rationale: FortiSandboxCloud is a cloud-based SaaS sandbox service from Fortinet accessed via web browser or integrated through FortiClient; there is no standalone macOS application or installer to deploy on a test VM.

fortinet/fortisandboxpaas.rationale: FortiSandbox PaaS is a cloud-based malware sandboxing service (Platform as a Service) hosted by Fortinet — it has no macOS client or installable component. It is accessed as a cloud subscription, not deployed on end-user machines.

fortinet/fortiweb.rationale: FortiWeb is an enterprise web application firewall (WAF) available only as a hardware appliance, VM image, or cloud service — not as a macOS desktop application. It is not available via Homebrew and has no macOS installer.

frangoteam/fuxa.rationale: FUXA is a Node.js web-based SCADA/HMI server application with no Homebrew formula or cask, and GitHub releases have no attached binary assets (.pkg or .dmg). The only macOS options are npm global install or GitHub Actions CI artifacts (requiring login), neither suitable for automated macOS VM testing.

franklioxygen/mytube.rationale: MyTube by franklioxygen is a self-hosted, Docker-based web application for downloading and playing YouTube/Bilibili content — not a native macOS desktop app. There is no Homebrew cask/formula, .pkg, or .dmg installer; it is deployed via Docker containers and is not appropriate for a macOS desktop inventory scan.

frappe/erpnext.rationale: ERPNext is a server-side web ERP platform requiring MariaDB, Redis, Python, and Node.js; it has no Homebrew cask/formula, no .pkg/.dmg installer, and is not a macOS desktop application that would appear in a typical Fleet customer's device inventory.

frappe/frappe.rationale: Frappe is a Python/JavaScript server-side web framework requiring MariaDB, Redis, and Node.js — it is not a macOS desktop application and would not appear in Fleet device inventory. No Homebrew formula or cask exists for it.

freescout/freescout.rationale: FreeScout is a self-hosted PHP/MySQL web application (help desk server software), not a macOS desktop application. It has no Homebrew cask or formula, and is not appropriate for macOS desktop installation testing.

frentix/openolat.rationale: OpenOLAT is a web-based LMS server application that runs on Linux/Tomcat/Java infrastructure and has no macOS desktop installer. It is not appropriate for macOS desktop installation.

frigate/frigate.rationale: Frigate is a Docker-containerized Linux NVR server application with no native macOS installer; it is not distributed as a Homebrew cask/formula, .pkg, or .dmg and is not appropriate for a macOS desktop inventory scan.

froxlor/froxlor.rationale: Froxlor is a Linux-only server management panel (officially supports Debian 12 and Ubuntu 24.04) with no Homebrew formula or cask, no macOS installer, and no relevance to macOS desktop deployments. It is not appropriate for a macOS test VM.

funadmin/funadmin.rationale: FunAdmin is a Chinese PHP-based backend admin framework (ThinkPHP8 + Layui) — a server-side web application framework, not a macOS desktop or CLI tool. It is not available via Homebrew and is not appropriate for macOS desktop installation.

funambol/zefiro.rationale: Funambol Zefiro is a mobile-only personal cloud app (iOS/Android) for photo/video backup, with no macOS desktop version, Homebrew formula, or direct installer available.

nyariv/sandboxjs.rationale: @nyariv/sandboxjs is a JavaScript library published on npm, not a standalone macOS application or CLI tool. It has no Homebrew formula/cask, no .pkg/.dmg installer, and is consumed as a dependency within Node.js projects.

oobabooga/text_generation_web_ui.rationale: text-generation-webui is a Python-based local LLM web application with no Homebrew formula or cask, and its macOS releases are only distributed as .tar.gz portable archives — no .pkg or .dmg installer exists.

open-emr/openemr.rationale: OpenEMR is a web-based PHP/LAMP electronic medical records application with no native macOS installer (.pkg/.dmg), no Homebrew formula or cask, and no standalone macOS desktop app — macOS installation requires manually deploying it on a local XAMPP/Apache stack, making it unsuitable for automated macOS desktop testing.

open5gs/open5gs.rationale: Open5GS is a 5G Core and LTE EPC server implementation (telecom infrastructure software), not a macOS desktop application. It has no Homebrew formula or cask, and macOS installation requires building from source — it would not appear in typical Fleet customer desktop inventories.

openairinterface/oai-cn5g-amf.rationale: OAI CN5G AMF is a 5G core network Access and Mobility Management Function that runs as Docker containers on Linux (Ubuntu/Red Hat). It is Linux-only server infrastructure with no macOS support, no Homebrew formula, and no macOS installer available.

opencc/jflow.rationale: JFlow (opencc/JFlow) is a Chinese AI-powered low-code BPM/workflow engine that runs as a Java server-side web application; it is not a macOS desktop application and is unlikely to appear in any Fleet customer's macOS device inventory.

openlit/openlit_software_development_kit.rationale: OpenLIT is a Python/TypeScript/Go SDK library installed via pip/npm — it has no standalone macOS installer, no Homebrew formula or cask, and is not a desktop application.

opennav/nav2.rationale: Nav2 (Navigation2) is a ROS2 robotics navigation framework primarily targeting Linux systems, installed via apt package manager. It is not available via Homebrew and has no macOS-native installer; it is not software that would appear in a typical Fleet customer's macOS device inventory.

openproject/openproject.rationale: OpenProject is a Linux-only server application with no macOS installer; its official docs explicitly state macOS is not supported as a native install target, with Docker or cloud as the only options.

openremote/openremote.rationale: OpenRemote is a server-side IoT platform that runs exclusively via Docker containers; it has no macOS desktop application, Homebrew package, or native installer. It is not the kind of software that would appear in a Fleet endpoint inventory.

openreplay/openreplay.rationale: OpenReplay is a self-hosted session replay server that runs on Linux via Kubernetes or Docker; it has no macOS desktop installer and is not distributed as a Homebrew package. Any 'macOS app' is just a WebCatalog wrapper around the web UI, not a native installable product matching this CPE.

openslides/openslides.rationale: OpenSlides is a web-based meeting/assembly management server deployed via Docker containers; it has no Homebrew formula or cask, no macOS desktop installer, and is not the kind of software that would appear in a Fleet-managed macOS device inventory.

opensolution/quick.cart.rationale: Quick.Cart is a PHP-based web shopping cart script, not a native macOS application. It runs on a server (local or remote) and has no macOS installer, Homebrew package, or desktop app.

openstack/keystonemiddleware.rationale: OpenStack Keystonemiddleware is a Python server-side middleware library used to authenticate tokens in OpenStack services — it is not a macOS desktop application and has no Homebrew formula, cask, or installable macOS package.

openstack/vitrage.rationale: OpenStack Vitrage is a server-side Root Cause Analysis service for OpenStack cloud deployments, designed exclusively for Linux controller nodes. It has no macOS installer, Homebrew package, or desktop presence.

opentext/filr.rationale: OpenText Filr is an enterprise file-sync-and-share server that runs on Linux (SLES/OES); CVE-2026-3266 is a server-side Missing Authorization vulnerability. Although a macOS desktop sync client exists, it is a separate product and not what the NVD CPE targets.

openvessl/anchorr.rationale: Anchorr is a Node.js/Docker-based Discord bot server application with no macOS desktop installer, Homebrew cask, or formula. It is a home-media server service not appropriate for macOS desktop fleet inventory testing.

opexustech/ecase_ecomplaint.rationale: eCASE eComplaint is a government-focused EEO case management web application that installs exclusively on Windows Server (IIS). There is no macOS installer, Homebrew package, or desktop app.

opexustech/ecase_portal.rationale: eCase Portal is a Windows Server IIS web application (server-side software), with no macOS client or desktop install — users access it via browser. Not appropriate for a macOS desktop install recipe.

oracle/agile_product_lifecycle_managemen.rationale: Oracle Agile PLM for Process is a server-side enterprise web application requiring Windows/IIS and Oracle/SQL Server databases — no macOS desktop installer exists. Not appropriate for a macOS test VM.

oracle/http_server.rationale: Oracle HTTP Server is part of Oracle Fusion Middleware and is only available for Linux (Oracle Linux/RHEL); no macOS build exists, there is no Homebrew formula or cask, and all official installation documentation targets Linux platforms only.

oracle/identity_manager.rationale: Oracle Identity Manager is an enterprise identity governance server product that runs on Linux/UNIX and requires Oracle WebLogic and a full Java EE stack — it has no macOS desktop client or installer. There is no Homebrew formula/cask and no macOS download available.

oracle/okit.rationale: Oracle OKIT (OCI Designer Toolkit) ships a native macOS desktop app (.dmg) in its GitHub releases; v0.3.0 is the latest with separate arm64 and x64 DMGs. No Homebrew formula or cask exists; the DMG is unsigned so security warnings may appear during install.

orangecat/syntx.rationale: SyntX is a VS Code extension (not a standalone macOS app), is discontinued and archived, and has no Homebrew formula/cask, no .pkg/.dmg installer — only installable via the VS Code Marketplace or VSIX.

oretnom23/online_food_ordering_system.rationale: oretnom23's Online Food Ordering System is a PHP/MySQL server-side web application distributed as source code from SourceCodester, not a native macOS desktop application. oretnom23/pharmacy_point_of_sale_system.rationale: oretnom23's Pharmacy Point of Sale System is a PHP/SQLite web application hosted on SourceCodester, accessed via a browser at localhost — it is server-side web software with no macOS desktop installer, Homebrew package, or .pkg/.dmg distribution.

oretnom23/simple_responsive_tourism_webs.rationale: This is a PHP/MySQL web application hosted on SourceCodester, not a desktop application. It has no macOS installer and is not installable via Homebrew or any native package manager.

orpc/orpc.rationale: oRPC (@orpc/client) is a Node.js/TypeScript npm library for building type-safe APIs, not a macOS desktop or CLI application. There is no Homebrew formula or cask, and it is not distributed as a .pkg or .dmg installer.

orval/orval.rationale: Orval is a Node.js/npm CLI tool for generating TypeScript API clients from OpenAPI specs; it has no Homebrew formula or cask, and no standalone .pkg/.dmg installer — it is distributed exclusively via npm and is not a traditional macOS desktop application.

As a regional Chinese server-side WAF, it is not appropriate for macOS desktop inventory and is unlikely to appear in Fleet customer environments.

xiweicheng/teamwork_management_system.rationale: xiweicheng/tms is a Chinese open-source web application (Java/Spring backend, browser-based UI) for team communication and collaboration — it is a self-hosted server application, not a macOS desktop app, and is unlikely to appear in Fleet customer inventories.

xwiki/xwiki.rationale: XWiki is a Java-based server-side wiki/collaboration platform with no Homebrew formula, cask, or native macOS installer (.pkg/.dmg). It runs on Tomcat/Java and is deployed as a web application, making it server software not appropriate for macOS desktop inventory.

xygeni/xygeni-action.rationale: xygeni-action is a GitHub Actions workflow component (used as 'uses: xygeni/xygeni-action@v5' in CI pipelines), not a macOS desktop or CLI application. It runs inside GitHub-hosted CI runners and has no macOS installer, Homebrew formula, or downloadable package.

yonyou/ksoa.rationale: Yonyou KSOA is a Chinese enterprise ERP/integration platform (Space-Time Enterprise Information Integration) with no Homebrew presence and no macOS installer. It is a server-side Java web application targeted at Chinese enterprises, not a macOS desktop product likely found in Fleet customers' inventories.

youlai/youlai-mall.rationale: youlai-mall is a Chinese open-source e-commerce microservices platform built on Spring Boot/Spring Cloud — a server-side backend application with no macOS desktop installer. It is not available via Homebrew and is not appropriate for a macOS desktop inventory.

zhongyu09/openchatbi.rationale: OpenChatBI is a Python-based backend BI/LLM agent tool installed via `git clone` and `uv sync`; it has no macOS desktop app, no Homebrew formula or cask, and no .pkg/.dmg installer — it is a developer Python service unlikely to appear in Fleet customer device inventory.

sources_consulted: brew tap zitadel/tap && brew info zitadel/tap/zitadel
zitadel/zitadel.rationale: ZITADEL provides an official Homebrew tap at zitadel/tap with a formula that installs the CLI/server binary directly. The tap is active and current (v4.13.1), covering the CVE-affected versions.
